Japanese: １月　７日　午後１２時５２分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: January 7, 12:52 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: 今日の法廷‥‥どうだったのかな。
English: So how do you think the trial went this morning?

Japanese: “どう”って？
English: How do you think it went!?

Japanese: なんだか、よけいに こんがらがっちゃったよ。
English: It got a bit crazy in there. I just wonder if that killed our chances.

Japanese: そうだなあ。あの、 メイワクなオジさんの証言‥‥
English: Yeah, I guess it did get out of hand. Mr. Kudo's testimony did nothing to help us.

Japanese: コーヒーに“白いコナ”を入れた ウエイトレスは、ダレだったのか？
English: Plus, now we don't even know the identity of the waitress who laced the coffee.

Japanese: オジさん、エプロンのヒモと リボンしか見てなかったんだよね。
English: All we know is what old Mr. Kudo saw... The apron straps, and the ribbon.

Japanese: それに、コマクの破れた耳に イヤホンをしていた、被害者‥‥
English: And that the victim was wearing an earpiece when his eardrum was ruptured.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。世の中、ムジュン だらけだよねー、ホントに。
English: Talk about a terrifying case of contradictionitus.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥１つでも多くの コタエを見つけないとね。
English: Time to play doctor and find ourselves a cure then, huh?

Japanese: マコちゃんのためだもんね！ いっちょ、やるよ！
English: Yeah, we've gotta find one for Maggey, or she's going to have a terminal case of guilty!

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: 今は亡くなってしまった所長、 千尋さんの形見‥‥だ。
English: He's sort of a keepsake... Something to remember Mia by.

Japanese: 散らかりほうだいの事務所だけど、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: Sure, the office is a mess, but I never forget to water this little fella.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、うっすらと ホコリをかぶってるな。
English: ...Actually, I've neglected them for so long that they're covered in a layer of dust.

Japanese: たまには‥‥せめて 読むフリだけでもしてみるか。
English: I guess I should at least pretend to read them once in a while.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: I don't get to use it much, so it's still super neat and tidy from when Pearls cleaned it.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: 《ホテル・バンドー》 超ゴウカな超一流ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: なんでも、となり街の一角を ゴッソリ買収して、
English: The chain is getting so rich that they bought a whole chunk of the next town over...

Japanese: 超レジャー施設《バンドーランド》 の建設が始まったらしい。
English: ...and started building a huge theme park. It's going to be called "Gatewater Land".

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、あのボーイさん から年賀状が来てたっけ。
English: ...I wonder if that bellboy is going to send me a greeting card this year, too?

Japanese: 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: ムカシ千尋さんが見て、 初めて泣いた映画なのだそうだ。
English: Apparently, this was the first movie that made Mia cry when she saw it, a long time ago.

Japanese: 最近、妹の真宵ちゃんが見て、 一晩中泣いていたらしい。
English: Maya watched it recently, and said she cried all night, too!

Japanese: ‥‥ということで、最近は このポスターを張ってある。
English: ...Which, I guess, is why it's back up on the wall.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみるか。
English: I'll have to check it out one of these days.

Japanese: さて‥‥、どこから手を つけたらいいかな。
English: Well? I'm officially at a loss as to where to start.

Japanese: そうだねえ。 なるほどくんはどう思う？
English: Yeah, me too. Let's try some brainstorming. You go first.

Japanese: とにかく、あのオジさんの証言を どうにかしたいよね。
English: I guess we should try to put Mr. Kudo's testimony to some sort of use.

Japanese: ふんふん。
English: Yeah, that's true.

Japanese: それに、被害者にコーヒーを運んだ ウエイトレスと、被害者自身‥‥
English: And we need to figure out the identity of the waitress and who the victim really was...

Japanese: やっぱり、事件のカギは 吐麗美庵にあるんじゃないかな。
English: Somehow, I think the key to this case has got to be at Trés Bien.

Japanese: よし！　じゃあ、 それで行ってみよう！
English: Well then, let's go back there and check it out again.

Japanese: あ！　あと、マコちゃんを はげましてあげないとね！
English: Oh! And we should drop in on Maggey and see how she's doing, too.

Japanese: ‥‥どう？　何か 気づいたこと、あるかな。
English: So... Anything on your mind?

Japanese: ああ‥‥そういえば。
English: Actually, there is something...

Japanese: ケッキョク、どうなったのかな。 なるほどくんのニセモノ。
English: I was wondering about Xin Eohp... You know, like what he's like and stuff.

Japanese: あ‥‥ 忘れてたな、そのコト。
English: Oh, yeah. I almost forgot about that guy.

Japanese: ボテくりこかしまわしてさらにコレ シバきたおしたろかいワレェェッ！
English: I atta beat you so hard, it'll feel like youse were smoochin' the express train!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ィィィ？ アンタがかァ？
English: Phoenix Wright? Youse sayin' YOU' Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: 弁護士の成歩堂　龍一ゆうたら‥‥ この、ワイのコトやああぁぁぁッ！
English: Cause I'M Phoenix Wright. The one and only!

Japanese: じつは‥‥ココロ当たりは あるんだよ。ソイツのこと。
English: Actually... I've learned a little something about my doppelganger.

Japanese: あれ。知ってるの？ ニセなるほどくん。
English: Huh? You did? What did you find out?

Japanese: （そうか‥‥。真宵ちゃんは アルバイト中だったっけ）
English: (Oh, yeah. Maya was working at the restaurant when I ran into Don Foneeonay...)

Japanese: ‥‥アレでニセモノだったら、 名誉キソンで訴えてやりたいよ。
English: Let's just say he was such a terrible version of me that I wanted to sue for defamation!

Japanese: （アイツの狙いは‥‥ いったい、なんなんだ？）
English: (What's that guy's story, anyway? What does he have to gain by impersonating me?)

Japanese: 今日のゴドー検事は いいキミだったねー。
English: Godot got it good today, huh?

Japanese: なんてったって、 ケムリ吹いてたもんね、最後。
English: He was even smoking at the end there!

Japanese: でも、たぶん明日には 新しい証人を用意してくるよ。
English: He'll have a new witness ready for tomorrow, though. You can count on it.

Japanese: （それまでに‥‥ 真相をつかむしかない！）
English: (We need to figure out the facts before then, or we're doomed!)

Japanese: また、助けてもらっちゃったな。 千尋さんには。
English: Mia really bailed me out again yesterday.

Japanese: まったく、シツレイしちゃうよ、 マメのオジさん。
English: Argh! That seedy old man was so rude to me!

Japanese: あたしのスゴさが わからないなんてねー。
English: He didn't realize just how awesome I am. If he had, I bet he would've treated me better.

Japanese: す、“スゴさ”ねえ‥‥
English: Since when did you become so awesome?

Japanese: どうなの？　霊媒師の 修行のほうは。
English: So? How's your spirit medium training going?

Japanese: ちょっと、お休み。 なんかね。ダメなんだ、今。
English: Well, I'm kind of taking a break... I'm having a bit of trouble right now, you know?

Japanese: （１年前の、あの事件から‥‥）
English: (Last year's incident must still be on her mind...)

Japanese: （修行している真宵ちゃんを 見なくなった）
English: (I haven't seen Maya train at all since then.)

Japanese: （“迷っている”‥‥千尋さんが そう言ってたっけ‥‥）
English: (I think Mia said that it's because Maya's "at a loss these days"...)

Japanese: 今日のイトノコさん、 カワイソウだったね。
English: I really felt sorry for Gumshoe today.

Japanese: マコちゃんを助けたいのに、 どんどん追いつめちゃって。
English: He really wanted to help Maggey out, but he kept getting backed into a corner.

Japanese: マコちゃんも キズついてたみたいだからなあ。
English: Maggey took it pretty bad, didn't she? Do you think she feels betrayed?

Japanese: 元気づけてあげないとね。 トモダチなんだから。
English: Maybe. We've got to cheer her up somehow. She's a friend, after all.

Japanese: （と、トモダチか‥‥ あまり考えたこと、なかったな）
English: (A friend? Hmm... Never really thought of it that way, but I guess she is.)

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、ミョーなムジュンが 出てきたでしょ。ウジャっと。
English: A lot of strange conflicting things kept popping up today in the trial, huh?

Japanese: たしかに、出てきたね。 ウジャっと。
English: Yeah, and they seemed to come out of nowhere.

Japanese: だから、マコちゃんにも ハナシを聞いとかないとね！
English: That's why we've gotta go talk with Maggey and get this whole thing straightened out!

Japanese: （マコちゃんは、被害者に コーヒーを運んだとき‥‥）
English: (Maggey said that when she brought the victim his coffee...)

Japanese: （“もう１人の客”を 目撃している‥‥）
English: (...there was one more person sitting at the table.)

Japanese: （そのムジュンも ハッキリさせないとな‥‥）
English: (This single contradiction is probably the most important one we have to resolve...)

Japanese: そういえば‥‥
English: Have you noticed the victims in our cases tend to be people we know nothing about, Nick?

Japanese: 今回も、ナゾのヒトだよね、 被害者って。
English: Take Glen Elg, for example.

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: Now that you mention it...

Japanese: どういうヒトで、どうして 殺害されたのか‥‥？
English: We don't know what kind of guy he was, or why he was killed.

Japanese: そろそろ、キチンと 調べないとね。
English: I say it's time to get out there and find some answers!

Japanese: マメのオジさんか。 名前、なんだっけ。
English: It's the seedy old man! What's his name again?

Japanese: イガラシ　マメゾウ、だったかな。 ‥‥ハッキリしないけど。
English: Vermin Kudo, is it? I can't remember, either.

Japanese: あそこまで法廷をメチャクチャに した証人は、初めてだろうね。
English: I've never seen a witness mess up so badly in court before.

Japanese: しばらく会わないほうが いいと思うよ。なるほどくんは。
English: Better stay away from him for a while, huh?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥。ナニを 食らわされるかわからない）
English: (Yeah. It might be more than seed that he chucks at me next time.)

Japanese: 店長さんも、目撃してる ワケでしょ？　事件は。
English: The chef's a witness too, right?

Japanese: そりゃそうだろうね。 ‥‥キッチンにいたんだから。
English: I suppose so. He was in the kitchen at the time of the incident.

Japanese: マメのオジさんの証言が ダメになっちゃったワケだから、
English: Well, since Old Man Seeds's testimony is pretty much useless to us...

Japanese: もう一度、ハナシを 聞いたほうがいいよね、店長さん。
English: ...I guess we should speak to Mr. Armstrong again, huh?

Japanese: どうかな。このヒトなんだけど。
English: Do you know anything about this person?

Japanese: うーん‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: さすがのあたしも、世界中のみんな とトモダチ、ってワケじゃないし。
English: Even someone as worldly-wise as me doesn't know everything about everyone, Nick.

Japanese: そのヒトについては、なんとも 言えないな。さすがのあたしも。
English: So I, the great Maya, am sad to say I can't tell you much about this individual.

Japanese: （そこまでキタイしてない）
English: (A simple yes or no, Maya... It's not as if you had to give me a dissertation...)

Japanese: アテにならないよねー、 そのバッジ。
English: That badge is totally useless, Nick.

Japanese: 何が？
English: Huh!?

Japanese: だって、ニセモノのなるほどくん、 バレなかったじゃない。
English: I mean, no one suspected that Xin Eohp wasn't a real lawyer.

Japanese: バッジがなくても。
English: I bet he didn't even have an attorney's badge!

Japanese: ‥‥言われてみれば、 そうだよな。
English: ...I guess you're right.

Japanese: あ。もしかして、スリかえられて いるんじゃないの、それ。
English: Or maybe he did have one! And maybe he switched your real one for a "fugazzi"!

Japanese: ま、まさかなあ。あはははは。
English: My badge? A fake!? ... Ha ha ha... That's a good one, Maya...

Japanese: 笑い声がカワいてるよ、 なるほどくん。
English: Why the nervous laughter? ...Aha! I knew it! Your badge really IS fake, isn't it Nick?

Japanese: ニセモノのなるほどくんかあ。
English: I can't believe there's a fake Nick running around.

Japanese: コイツの目的は、 いったいなんだったのかね。
English: Yeah, I know. What's the deal with that? Why's he impersonating me?

Japanese: やっぱり、なるほどくんに ウラミがあったんじゃないの？
English: Maybe he's got something against you.

Japanese: ある種、キラワレ者じゃない、 なるほどくんって。
English: People do tend to have a distinct distaste for you, Nick.

Japanese: （まあ、検事には毎回 イジめられてるよな‥‥）
English: (True enough... The only thing prosecutors seem to like about me is pushing me around...)

Japanese: それか‥‥マコちゃんに ウラミがあったのかな。
English: Or... Maybe he's got something against Maggey.

Japanese: （マコちゃんを有罪にするため、 ニセモノを演じたワケか‥‥）
English: (So he pretended to be me to get Maggey convicted?)

Japanese: このラクガキ‥‥ 被害者が書いたんだよね。
English: This doodle here... It was drawn by the victim, right?

Japanese: なんと言っても、 事件当日の新聞だからなあ。
English: This is a newspaper from the day of the incident, so, yeah, we've got to assume so.

Japanese: きっと、何かの手がかりに なると思うんだけど。
English: There must be a clue in there somewhere.

Japanese: いろんなヒトに 見てもらおうか、とりあえず。
English: I guess we should show it around and see if anyone can tell us more about it.

Japanese: ５０００万円の借金かあ。
English: A loan of half a million dollars!

Japanese: 何をどうすれば、そんなに 借金できるのかなあ。
English: I wonder what Mr. Armstrong did to get into that much debt.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんも、 ムダ使いしちゃだめだぞ。
English: I'm going to guess he spent money needlessly. You'd better watch your spending, too.

Japanese: あ！　こうしたらどうかな。
English: Hey, I've got a great idea!

Japanese: “ニクハチセット”を ２９８００円にするとか！
English: What if Mr. Armstrong charged $20,000 for the Twin-T set?

Japanese: ‥‥暴動が起こるぞ、 そんなコトしたら。
English: If you hit people with that kind of bill, Maya, they will hit you back!

Japanese: どこかにぶつけたのかな。 ハデにポンコツだね。
English: That thing must've been in an accident. It's a total wreck!

Japanese: 公園で会った男が 乗っていたスクーターなんだけど。
English: That's what the guy I met at the park was riding. And I'm betting he's the fake me.

Japanese: ぐわああああああっはっはっはっ はっはっはっはっはっはっはァァ！
English: Gwoaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaar!

Japanese: 弁護士の成歩堂　龍一ゆうたら‥‥ この、ワイのコトやああぁぁぁッ！
English: Cause I'M Phoenix Wright. The one and only!

Japanese: あのデカい声‥‥ まだ耳がキンキンしてるよ。
English: His voice is still ringing in my ears.

Japanese: 今度、紹介してね。 ニセなるほどくん。
English: Introduce me next time, Nick! I want to meet Xin Eohp, too!

Japanese: 毒が入っていた カップだね、それ。
English: That's the cup the poison was in, isn't it?

Japanese: たしかに、右手で飲んだときの 跡だよなあ。
English: Yeah, and the stain on the rim shows that the victim used his right hand to pick it up.

Japanese: でも、オジさんが見た 被害者は‥‥左手で飲んでいた。
English: But the old man swore he saw the victim pick it up with his left hand...

Japanese: （見まちがいでないとすれば、 考えられる可能性は‥‥？）
English: (If Mr. Kudo wasn't mistaken, then what could account for this contradiction...?)

Japanese: あたしも買おうかなあ、宝クジ。
English: Maybe I should try the lottery sometime, too.

Japanese: いいんじゃないかな。 ５０００万円だもんね、当たれば。
English: Sure, why not? If you hit the jackpot, you could win half a million bucks.

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。買って。
English: So, what are you waiting for, Nick? Buy one!

Japanese: それはムシがよすぎるだろう！
English: No way! Don't you know the lottery is a tax on people who are bad at math!

Japanese: とりあえず、“お金のたまる方法” に書き加えないとね。
English: Blah. Fine then. At least I can add it to the newly renamed "Paths to Wealth"!

Japanese: あとで、はみちゃんに 電話しようっと。
English: I'll have to call Pearly later and have her add, "Buy lottery tickets whenever possible."

Japanese: 《ニューイヤー耳鼻科》かあ。
English: "New Ear Otolaryngological Clinic". Hmm...

Japanese: コマクが破れたら、やっぱり ラジオは聞けないよねえ。
English: I guess if your eardrum was ruptured, you couldn't listen to the radio, huh?

Japanese: まあ、イヤホンは つけないだろうね。
English: Yeah. It'd be pretty pointless to wear an earpiece of any kind.

Japanese: この袋に入っていたお薬も 消えちゃったんだよね。
English: The medication in the bag is missing too, right?

Japanese: （‥‥消えたクスリ、か）
English: (The mysterious vanishing medication... Much like our mysterious "other person"...)

Japanese: うーん。ここまで来ると、 ゲージュツ的だよね。
English: It's almost surreal that this artsy apron is what got us this far.

Japanese: 一度見たら、忘れないだろうな。
English: Well, as art goes, this piece definitely leaves an impression.

Japanese: やっぱり、あのオジさんは 見ていなかったんだね。
English: I guess that means the old guy never saw the front of it, huh?

Japanese: ウエイトレスさんのカオ‥‥。
English: Which means he didn't see the waitress's face, either...

Japanese: これが、マコちゃんの エプロンのポケットから‥‥
English: I can't believe they found this in Maggey's pocket.

Japanese: まあ、マコちゃんは キゼツしていたから、
English: Yeah, but she was passed out, right?

Japanese: ハンニンが入れたと 考えられるけどね。
English: So the killer could've easily planted it on her.

Japanese: でも‥‥ハンニンは どこで買ってきたのかな。
English: Where do you even get a hold of something like this?

Japanese: こんなモードク。
English: It's not like you can go down to your local CostMo' and buy it in bulk for $5 a pound!

Japanese: （入手方法か‥‥。 それもダイジだな、たしかに）
English: (She has got a point... How the killer got a hold of this poison is pretty important.)

Japanese: オジさんの反省文だね。
English: A letter of apology from the old man? Aww, how sweet.

Japanese: ‥‥いちおう“証言” なんだけどね、これは。
English: Actually, it's his "testimony"... If you can call it that.

Japanese: 『花ビンを割りました。 ごめんなさい』‥‥かあ。
English: So? What's it say? ..."I broke the vase at my seat. I'm sorry."?

Japanese: あたしも小さいころは パリンパリン割ったよ。いろいろ。
English: Heh heh! I used to break things all the time when I was little, you know.

Japanese: そして、バリバリ書いたね。 反省文。
English: And I used to have to write apologies all the time, too.

Japanese: （春美ちゃんや霧緒さんも パリンパリン割ってるしな）
English: (Well, Pearls and Adrian seem to have picked up where you left off...)

Japanese: どうかな。これなんだけど。
English: Do you know anything about this, Maya?

Japanese: うーん‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: さすがのあたしも、この世のすべて を知ってるワケじゃないからねー。
English: Even someone as worldly-wise as me doesn't know everything about everything, Nick.

Japanese: それについては、なんとも 言えないな。さすがのあたしも。
English: So I, the great Maya, am sad to say I can't tell you much about this object.

Japanese: （そこまでキタイしてない）
English: (A simple yes or no, Maya... It's not as if you had to give me a dissertation...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: January 7 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 取り調べ中だってさ、 マコちゃん。
English: I guess Maggey's still in questioning.

Japanese: えー！　話を聞きたかったのに。
English: But! But we've got questions to ask her, too!

Japanese: マー　コー　ちゃん！
English: Magg-ey! Magg-ey!

Japanese: や、やめろよ！　遊びに来た コドモじゃないんだから。
English: Keep it down, Maya! This isn't a playground, you know!

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 今は、することがないのだろう。 立ったまま寝ている。
English: There's nothing for him to do right now, so he's catching some vertical z's.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: January 7 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あッ、成歩堂さん！
English: Oh, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥。 取り調べ、終わったんだね。
English: Hello, Maggey. So they finished questioning you?

Japanese: もおスズキ、 メチャカンドーッス！
English: Wasn't it just unbelievable in court today, sir!?

Japanese: 今日の裁判のコト、テツヤで マコ日記に書きまくるッス！
English: I'm going to stay up all night and blog about everything that happened!

Japanese: 今日はアブなかったねー、 マコちゃん！
English: Weren't you scared? It was pretty touch and go in there.

Japanese: いやー。あのオジさん、イイ やられっぷりだったッス。
English: Yeah, but you totally nailed that old man!

Japanese: メチャクチャだったからね。 ‥‥あの証言は。
English: Well, he was all over the place with his testimony.

Japanese: でも‥‥、メチャクチャなのは このスズキもいっしょッス。
English: He's not the only one!
Huh? What do you mean?

Japanese: あまりにも、食いちがってる コトが多すぎるッス。
English: Everyone else's testimonies don't match up either.

Japanese: みんなが“見た”事件と、 アタシのキオク‥‥。
English: Not with what I remember of the incident, anyway...
(Is it possible she is the one misremembering things...?)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: ウエイトレスさんがめずらしい のだろうか。
English: I bet they don't get too many waitresses in here.

Japanese: ちょっと そわそわしている。
English: He looks like he's getting a bit restless.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥ さっき言ってたよね。
English: Maggey... You know how you said that everyone else...

Japanese: みんなの見たコトと キオクが食いちがう‥‥って。
English: ...provided testimony that didn't match up with what you remembered?

Japanese: はいッ！　いろいろあるケド‥‥ ヤッパリ、いちばんデッカイのは、
English: Yup. There are just so many things that don't seem to add up.

Japanese: ‥‥被害者のテーブルにいた、 《もう１人の男》ッス。
English: The biggest contradiction is the other guy I saw at the victim's table.

Japanese: コーヒーに、何か入れたのは‥‥ あのオトコでした！
English: HE was the one who slipped something into the victim's coffee. I'm sure it was him!

Japanese: （今日の法廷では、五十嵐さんが 証言していたな‥‥）
English: (But didn't Mr. Kudo testify earlier today...)

Japanese: （“白いコナを入れたのは ウエイトレスだった”‥‥って）
English: (...that it was the waitress who put some white powder into the coffee cup...?)

Japanese: 消えた“もう１人のオトコ” ‥‥か。
English: So you really think it was this "disappearing man" that did it...?

Japanese: “消えた”といえば、もう１つ。 ＣＤがあるッス。
English: Well, he's not the only thing that disappeared. The CD vanished as well.

Japanese: サインペンでバンドの名前が 書いてあった‥‥
English: You know, the CD with the writing on it.

Japanese: ああ。《クリキントン》ね。
English: Oh, yeah. The "MC Screwdriver" album, right?

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》だよ。 スポーツ新聞にも書いてあったろ。
English: It was "MC Bomber", Maya. That name was scrawled on the sports paper as well.

Japanese: ‥‥そのＣＤも、現場からは 見つからなかったッス。
English: They never did find that CD at the crime scene, sir.

Japanese: （たしか、被害者の薬袋の中身も 行方不明だったっけな‥‥）
English: (Or the victim's medication. That's gone missing, too.)

Japanese: うううう‥‥ ワケわかんないよ、もう。
English: Ouch, my head... This is getting way too complicated for me!

Japanese: 岡　高夫が倒れたとき‥‥ たしか、キゼツしたんだよね？
English: You said that you passed out when the victim, Glen Elg, collapsed, right?

Japanese: お恥ずかしいハナシです。 元・婦人警官としては。
English: Yes. It's so embarrassing. I mean, I used to be a cop!

Japanese: 気がついたときには、 もう店には警察官があふれていて。
English: When I came to, the restaurant was buzzing with police.

Japanese: ‥‥さっさとタイホされたッス。
English: And before I knew what was going on, they arrested me, sir.

Japanese: 事件が起こってから、 警察が来るまでのあいだ‥‥
English: So between the time the victim collapsed and the time the police arrived on the scene...

Japanese: 吐麗美庵で何が起こっても、 きみにはわからなかったワケだね？
English: ...you have no idea what went on at Trés Bien.

Japanese: そ、そッスけど。 どうしたッスか？　成歩堂さん。
English: No. No idea at all. Why? Is it important, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 事件を目撃したオジさん‥‥ 彼は、店を追い出されてるんだ。
English: The other witness, the old man from the park, was pretty much chased out of the picture.

Japanese: お、追い出された？
English: "Chased out of the picture"? What do you mean?

Japanese: 店長さんに《警察を呼んでくれ》 って、たのまれたんだって。
English: Old Seedy wasn't inside the restaurant because he was told to go call the police.

Japanese: そう。‥‥そしてきみは、 キゼツしていた。
English: Exactly. And you, Maggey, were unconscious.

Japanese: ‥‥吐麗美庵には、本土坊　薫しか いなかったことになる。
English: That means Mr. Armstrong was alone in the restaurant for a brief period of time.
No...

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥店長が、 アタシに‥‥ツミを‥‥？
English: Y-You don't think Mr. Armstrong... set me up... do you?

Japanese: 状況を考えると‥‥
English: When you consider the facts...

Japanese: あの店長が事件に無関係だとは、 考えにくいんじゃないかな。
English: ...it's hard to imagine that Mr. Armstrong isn't involved in this at all.

Japanese: うううう‥‥かい主に噛まれた イヌみたいなキモチッス‥‥。
English: Grrr! It's like the master biting the paw of the dog that it feeds!

Japanese: ‥‥そのハナシ‥‥ ホントなんですか？　成歩堂さん。
English: Are you sure about this, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: さっき、オジさん本人に 聞いてきたばっかりだからね。
English: Well, the old man said as much when we spoke with him earlier.

Japanese: ‥‥どうも、しっくり来ないッス。 その、オジさんのハナシ‥‥。
English: I don't know. The things that man says don't add up for some reason, sir...

Japanese: （マコちゃん、 考えこんでるな‥‥）
English: (Maggey looks like she's trying to figure something out...)

Japanese: オジさんのコト‥‥詳しく 聞いてみたほうがいいのかなあ。
English: We'd better find out exactly what happened to the old man on that day.

Japanese: 何か、気になることでも あるの？　五十嵐さんのこと。
English: Is there anything about Mr. Kudo's testimony that stood out as odd to you?

Japanese: じつは‥‥あるッス。
English: Actually... Yes.

Japanese: そもそも‥‥あのオジさんが 証言してるコト自体が、ちょっと。
English: The fact that he was even testifying to begin with doesn't quite...

Japanese: ‥‥？　どういうコト？
English: "Doesn't quite" what?

Japanese: スズキが、あのテーブルに コーヒーを運んだとき‥‥
English: Well, when I took the coffee over to the victim's table...

Japanese: 店内には、たしかに お客さんがもう１人、いたッス。
English: ...it's true there was another customer in the restaurant.

Japanese: （それが、五十嵐さんだった、 ってワケだよな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, we know that already. It was Victor Kudo.)

Japanese: でも、それは‥‥ オジさんじゃなかったッス。
English: But I can't really say it was an old man.

Japanese: おジイさんだった、とか！
English: OK, then how about calling him a really old middle-aged man!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなササイな ちがいじゃないッス。
English: No, age isn't the issue.

Japanese: たしか‥‥オンナのヒト、 だったような。
English: The other customer was a woman.

Japanese: お、オンナ‥‥‥？
English: A woman!?

Japanese: ホントなの！　マコちゃん！
English: Are you sure, Maggey!?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。ちょっと、 自信はないッスけど‥‥。
English: Well, I'm not a hundred percent sure, but I think so.

Japanese: で！　で！　どんなヒトだったの？ その、オンナのヒト！
English: So? What did this woman look like?

Japanese: うう‥‥たしか、ちょっと ジメッとしていて‥‥
English: Umm... She was sort of creepy...

Japanese: イミありげに“ククッ‥‥”と 笑っていたような。
English: And she had a kind of cackling laugh.

Japanese: “ジメッと”‥‥？
English: "Creepy"?

Japanese: “ククッ”と‥‥？
English: "Cackling"?

Japanese: （最近どこかで、そんなオンナの ヒトを、見かけたような‥‥）
English: (Why do I get the feeling I've come across a woman like that recently?)

Japanese: 去年は、お世話になりました！
English: Thanks for your help last year!

Japanese: よかったよね！ この前の事件、ムザイになって。
English: Aw, it was nothing. I'm glad we got you off the hook.

Japanese: 今年もよろしくおねがいします！
English: I hope you can help me out this year, too!

Japanese: ‥‥ムザイになるといいね。 今回の事件も。
English: Yeah, me too. It'd be sweet if we could get that verdict overturned.

Japanese: 警官時代は、さんざん メンドウを見てもらったッス。
English: Back when I was an officer, Detective Gumshoe always looked out for me.

Japanese: ‥‥しかァし！
English: But today!

Japanese: 今日は、さんざんな目に あわせてもらったッス！
English: Today I was the one who had to look out for him; trying to incriminate me the whole time!

Japanese: い、いや。でも、イトノコさん、 刑事だからね‥‥
English: You've got to remember, Maggey. Gumshoe's a detective. He's got a job to do.

Japanese: センパイだけは‥‥
English: My old boss...

Japanese: 自分の味方になってくれると 思っていたッス。
English: I thought at least he'd be on my side.

Japanese: 味方だよ！　イトノコさんは。 だって、マコちゃんのために、必死
English: He is on your side! He'd do anything to help y--

Japanese: いーや、ダマされないッス！ 今日の法廷でハッキリしたッス！
English: You can't fool me! I saw him in court today!

Japanese: 親キツツキにつつかれた 子キツツキみたいなキモチッス！
English: I felt like a poor little baby woodpecker being pecked on the head by its own mother!

Japanese: （今日のイトノコ刑事の証言‥‥ スキがなかったからな）
English: (Gumshoe's testimony was pretty solid... No wonder it hurt her so much.)

Japanese: もー、キライッス！ カオも見たくないッス！
English: I hate him, sir! I mean it! I don't ever want to see him again!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Ouch. I think I just saw Gumshoe's chances go down in flames like the Hindenburg...)

Japanese: なんとか、イトノコさんの キモチを伝えられないかな‥‥。
English: I don't think it's getting through to her that he really was trying to help...

Japanese: スズキ、どんなコトがあっても、 くじけないッス！
English: No matter what happens, I never let things get me down!

Japanese: いつも前向きだよねー、 マコちゃん。
English: You're always so positive, aren't you, Maggey?

Japanese: “マコ”という名前は、 “マけないコ”というイミッス！
English: Macho Maggey Byrde, they say! The early Byrde catches the worm! A Byrde in the hand!

Japanese: え。そうだったんだ。
English: Wow! All those phrases are named after you?

Japanese: そしてスズキ、どんなコトからも、 逃げないッス！
English: And I never turn my back on anything!

Japanese: いつも強気だしねー、 マコちゃん。
English: You're always so strong, aren't you, Maggey?

Japanese: “マコ”の名前のもう１つのイミは “マっコう勝負”ッス！
English: Macho Maggey Byrde, they say! I'm a tough old Byrde!

Japanese: “マっコう勝負”だって、 なるほどくん。
English: "Tough old byrde," Nick! Hee hee! Do you get it?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: ...Yeah, I get it.

Japanese: あ‥‥そのヒト、 亡くなった被害者ッスね。
English: Ah! That's the victim, isn't it?

Japanese: たしか、『初めて見た』って 言ってたよね。
English: That day was the first time you'd ever seen this man, right?

Japanese: そッス！　ゼンゼン 知らないヒトッス。
English: Yes. I'd never met or seen him before then.

Japanese: たまたまアタシが コーヒー運んだだけッス！
English: I just happened to be the waitress who served him his coffee, sir. That's all!

Japanese: ‥‥コンピュータの プログラマーらしいけど。
English: It seems he was a computer programmer.

Japanese: もお、そんな！　自分は、 キカイはゼンゼン、ダメッス！
English: Really? I'm useless with computers. Completely hopeless.

Japanese: “ぷらぐろまー”なんて 知り合い、いないッス！
English: I don't know any computer programs.

Japanese: （ビミョーにまちがってるな）
English: (Did she mean "programmers"?)

Japanese: ケーサツをクビになって、ヘコんで いたスズキを救ってくれたッス。
English: Mr. Armstrong was very good to me after I was fired from the force.

Japanese: まさか‥‥事件に関係してる、 なんてコトは‥‥
English: I can't believe someone as nice as him could have anything to do with this...

Japanese: （事件発生から、警察への 通報までの、数分間‥‥）
English: (Well, in the time between the victim being poisoned and the police being called...)

Japanese: （現場にいて、細工をする チャンスがあったのは‥‥）
English: (...the only person who was on the scene and had a chance to do anything...)

Japanese: （今のところ、本土坊さんしか 考えられないんだよな‥‥）
English: (...was Mr. Armstrong. There was no one else there.)

Japanese: まさか‥‥店長が‥‥店長が、 スズキに、ツミを‥‥！
English: M-Mr. Armstrong wouldn't... He wouldn't set me up... would he!?

Japanese: たしかに、このスズキ。 ムカシは婦人警官だったッス。
English: I know I used to be on the police force, sir.

Japanese: でも今は、とらわれの身。 なんの情報も入ってこないッス。
English: But I'm incarcerated now. So I can't use my connections to help you.

Japanese: 今の自分にわかるのは、前科者と 店の常連さんだけッス。
English: All I can tell you about now is info about ex-cons or the clientele of Trés Bien, sir...

Japanese: グチっぽくなっちゃったよ。 マコちゃん。
English: Aww, don't let it get you down, Maggey!

Japanese: まぁさしく、 そいつッスウウウゥゥゥ！
English: Hoooly smokes! That's him!!

Japanese: な‥‥ナニが？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 成歩堂さんのニセモノ！ その、ニヤけて日焼けたツラ！
English: That's your phony, Mr. Wright! Just look at that ridiculous suntan!

Japanese: ぼくは、ニヤけても日焼けても いないけど‥‥。
English: Um... For the record, I'm not sunburned like an over-dried tomato so I don't know how...

Japanese: 出張でサイパンに行って 日焼けした、と言ってたッス。
English: He told me he'd been on a business trip to Hawaii and that's where he got the tan.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (I'm not hearing this...)

Japanese: 弁護士バッジ‥‥ッスか？
English: Ah! Is that... your attorney's badge?

Japanese: ニセモノだけどね。
English: Actually, it's a fake.

Japanese: まぁさしく、 そいつッスウウウゥゥゥ！
English: Hoooly smokes! That's it!!

Japanese: な‥‥ナニが？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 成歩堂さんのニセモノが つけていたバッジ！
English: That's the badge your phony had, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥こんなのでダマされたの？
English: You got duped by this...!?

Japanese: 色がゼンゼンちがうよ？
English: But it's a completely different color!

Japanese: それ以前に、紙でできてるし。
English: And what about the fact that it's made of paper!?

Japanese: 出張でサイパンに行って、バッジも 日焼けした、と言ってたッス。
English: He said the badge got a tan as well, while he was in Hawaii on business.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (I'm beginning to see how my phony was able to gain her trust...)

Japanese: あ、そのバッジ！ ‥‥ホンモノッスか？
English: That badge! Is it real?

Japanese: ほ、ホンモノだよ！
English: Of course it's real!

Japanese: 一度ダマされたニンゲンは、 うたぐり深くなるッス。
English: That's what they all say. But I've been duped before.

Japanese: 貸してくださいッ！
English: Give it to me for a sec!

Japanese: ‥‥かりっ！
English: ...*chomp!*

Japanese: バッジを噛んでるよ。
English: She... She bit it...

Japanese: ちょっぴり歯形がついたッス。
English: And I left a few teeth marks, too!

Japanese: はあ。
English: I can see that...

Japanese: ‥‥それが何をイミするのか わからないのがツラいところッス。
English: I just wish I remembered if that means it's real or fake.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 （ホンモノなのに）
English: *sigh* (I said it was real!)

Japanese: そんなモードク、 聞いたこともないッス！
English: Potassium cyanide? I've never heard of that poison before in my life, sir!

Japanese: ま、まあ、それはウソッスけど。
English: OK, maybe I have.

Japanese: でも、アタシ‥‥そんな毒、 ホントに見たこともないッス。
English: But I've never seen it before. That's the truth.

Japanese: だいいち、青酸カリ屋さんが どこにあるかも知らないし。
English: I wouldn't even know where the nearest potassium cyanide dealer is in the first place.

Japanese: 青酸カリ屋さん‥‥
English: Potassium cyanide dealers? Sounds like another group of drug pushers to avoid...

Japanese: たしかに、気軽に買えるモノじゃ なさそうだよなあ。
English: In any case, this definitely isn't something you can pick up just anywhere.

Japanese: アタシがキゼツしてるあいだに、 ハンニンがツルッと入れたッス！
English: Ooh!! Curse my rotten luck!

Japanese: スズキのエプロンに！
English: The criminal must've stuck the bottle in my apron while I was unconscious!

Japanese: おぞましいキオクッス。 あの日の裁判‥‥
English: Just thinking about the trial that day gives me the creeps.

Japanese: そ、そうなの？
English: Was it that bad?

Japanese: ま、まあ。あくまでも ぼくのニセモノだからね！
English: D-Don't forget that was my phony, not me!

Japanese: 『真犯人は被告人じゃない！』 ‥‥と叫ぶべきところを、
English: He was supposed to say, "Surely the defendant isn't the real criminal!"

Japanese: 『真犯人は被告人じゃないか！』 ‥‥と言いまちがえてみたり。
English: But instead he shouted out, "Surely the defendant is the real criminal!" ...By mistake.

Japanese: そ、そうなの！
English: Oh, ouch.

Japanese: だ、だから。あくまでも ぼくのニセモノだからね！
English: L-Like I said, that was my phony, not me!

Japanese: もースズキ、 思い出したくないッス。 I...!
English: I don't want to think about it anymore...

Japanese: （ぼくだって 想像したくないぞ‥‥）
English: (Trust me, neither do I...)

Japanese: たしかに‥‥それをテーブルに 運んだのは、アタシッス。
English: That's the cup I took over to the table, sir.

Japanese: でも‥‥！
English: But!

Japanese: カップに白いコナを 入れるなんて‥‥
English: I didn't put anything in it...

Japanese: スズキに‥‥そんなコト、 できっこないッス！
English: I just... I could never do something like that!

Japanese: そうだよね！
English: That's right! You could never do anything like that!

Japanese: スズキ、トレイにモノを乗せて 運ぶのが、大のニガテッス。
English: I'm not good at carrying those large trays, you know.

Japanese: 片手でトレイを持って、カップに 何か入れるなんて‥‥
English: Trying to balance a tray with one hand to put something in the coffee with the other...

Japanese: そんなアブないコトしたら、 トレイをひっくり返しちゃうッス！
English: That'd just be asking for trouble. I'd drop something, for sure!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだよね！
English: Th-That's right!

Japanese: （“ブキヨウだ”と、 そう言いたいワケか）
English: (...I-Is she expecting me to claim that she's too clumsy to have committed the crime...?)

Japanese: モーニングセットを運んだとき、 やっちまったッス。
English: Oh, yeah. That's from when I was carrying a customer's breakfast over to them.

Japanese: このケチャップ？
English: The ketchup splotch, you mean?

Japanese: もお、カオじゅう まっかっかに。
English: My whole face was fire-engine red, thanks to that stuff.

Japanese: あれ‥‥？　でも、ケチャップを こぼしたの、エプロンでしょ？
English: But you spilled the ketchup on your apron, didn't you? I don't see how...

Japanese: 客のカオに、思うさま オムレツをたたきつけたッス。
English: The ketchup-covered omelet went flying and hit the customer in the face.

Japanese: ひゃー。見たかったなあ。 そのお客さんのカオ。
English: Oh, talk about a tomato-red face! Makes me wish I could've seen it myself.

Japanese: まあ、たしかに‥‥ 見せたかったッス。あのカオは。
English: Yeah, I guess... It was kind of a sight to behold.

Japanese: そうだ！　いいものがあるよ。
English: Oh, yeah! I've got something you're going to love!

Japanese: えッ！　なんスか、なんスか！
English: Really? What is it!?

Japanese: さし入れの、おベントウ。 ‥‥ホラ。
English: A lunchbox, just for you. Here!

Japanese: うわあ‥‥ソーゼツな ベントウッスねー。
English: Wow! A lunchbox! Weenies, too!

Japanese: でも、うれしいッス！　‥‥あの。 成歩堂さんが、わざわざ？
English: I can't believe it! Thank you, sir! Did you make this just for me, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ちがうよ。イトノコさんに 決まってるでしょ！
English: Nah, it was Detective Gumshoe! Who else would make such a nice lunchbox for you?

Japanese: イトノコセンパイ‥‥？
English: Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: マコちゃんのコトをシンパイして、 いっしょうけんめい作ったんだよ！
English: He's really worried about you. Looks like he put a lot of effort into making this, too!

Japanese: ザンネンッスけど‥‥ 留置所で、さし入れは禁止ッス！
English: I can't accept it. Detention Center rules. No gifts allowed, sir.

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥そんな、 カタいコト言わずに‥‥
English: Hey, come on, Maggey. Don't be like that.

Japanese: キソクはキソクッス。 守らなければ、タイホするッス。
English: The rules are the rules! They'll lock you up if you break them.

Japanese: （元・婦人警官のウエイトレスに 言われると、さすがにコワいな）
English: (Somehow, when an ex-cop turned waitress says that, it seems a whole lot scarier.)

Japanese: それに‥‥‥
English: And anyway...

Japanese: スズキ、ウインナーは 大ッキライッス！
English: I hate weenies!

Japanese: ‥‥そう、なんだ‥‥
English: O-Oh... really?

Japanese: 真宵さんにあげるッス！
English: It's all yours, Maya! You can enjoy it with Mr. Wright!

Japanese: で、でも‥‥
English: B-But...

Japanese: そうだな。悪くなるまえに 食べちゃおうか。
English: She's right... It's better than letting it go to waste.

Japanese: え！ ‥‥いいのかなあ。
English: But! ...I guess so.

Japanese: 《イトノコのベントウ》を 真宵ちゃんと食べた。
English: Gumshoe's Lunchbox eaten with Maya.

Japanese: あの。オイシイッスか？
English: Well? How was it?

Japanese: うん。 ウインナーは大好きだからね。
English: That hit the spot! I love weenies.

Japanese: そ、それはよかったッス！
English: Oh, good! I'm glad I gave it to you, then, sir!

Japanese: それ、被害者が持ってきた 新聞らしいッスね。
English: That's the paper the victim had with him, isn't it?

Japanese: そうみたいッスよ。
English: That's what we think, madam.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、新聞は あったような気がするッス。
English: I kind of remember him having a paper, actually.

Japanese: この《クリーニング・ボンバー》と いうヒビキも、おぼえがあるッス。
English: And I remember something about this "MC Bomber", too.

Japanese: 現場から消えたＣＤに 書いてあったんだよね。
English: It was written on the CD that disappeared as well, wasn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、ＣＤの１枚や２枚、 事件に関係ないとは思うッスケド。
English: I don't suppose it makes any difference if there was one CD or two, right?

Japanese: いやー、今日の法廷は スッキリしたッス！
English: Ah, I feel much better after the trial this morning.

Japanese: ずいぶん長いコト、法廷マニアを やってるッスが‥‥、
English: I've been a bit of a courtroom proceedings addict for years now.

Japanese: あんなにズルズルになった証人、 ひさしぶりッス。
English: It feels like forever since I saw a witness as slippery as that old man.

Japanese: ‥‥ベツに、マメのオジさんが 悪いワケじゃないんだけどね。
English: He's not really that bad of an old man though.

Japanese: ただ‥‥ミョーに スッキリしないッス。
English: Still, I feel a bit uneasy.

Japanese: え。‥‥でも、さっきは “スッキリした”って‥‥
English: Huh? I thought you just said you felt much better...

Japanese: ‥‥マコちゃん。
English: Maggey.

Japanese: 気になることがあったら、 なんでも言ってみてくれないかな。
English: If there's something on your mind, you've got to tell us.

Japanese: 五十嵐さんと‥‥ 彼の証言について。
English: Especially if it has anything to do with Mr. Kudo or his testimony.

Japanese: 了解ッス！　スズキ、 ゼンブ吐きだしてみるッス！
English: Roger! I'll spill it all and see what you make of it!

Japanese: たしかに、このスズキ。 ムカシは婦人警官だったッス。
English: I know I used to be in the police force.

Japanese: でも今は、とらわれの身。 なんの情報も入ってこないッス。
English: But I'm incarcerated now. I don't pick up much information in here.

Japanese: 今の自分にわかるのは、 レストランのメニューぐらいッス。
English: All I can help you out with now is the menu at Trés Bien.

Japanese: グチっぽくなっちゃったよ。 マコちゃん。
English: Hey, come on, Maggey. Don't let your spirits fall!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 バグダス
English: January 7 Blue Screens, Inc.

Japanese: うわ！　“はいてく”のカオリが するよ、なるほどくん！
English: Wow! This place is so hi-tech! You can almost smell the electricity in the air!

Japanese: まあ、コンピュータの 会社だからね。
English: It is a computer firm, Maya. They can't work without electricity, you know.

Japanese: どーしたのかなキミたちー。
English: Who are you?

Japanese: あ‥‥ こ、こんにちは‥‥
English: Oh, umm... Hello...

Japanese: んー、ダメだぞー。 勝手に入って来ちゃあ。
English: I'm sorry. Access is restricted to authorized personnel only.

Japanese: ここでは、コンピュータの プログラムを作ってるからねー。
English: This is a computer programming laboratory.

Japanese: すっごいヒミツが イッパイなんだよー。
English: There are far too many trade secrets that could be leaked.

Japanese: うわあ。ヒミツですか？
English: Wow! What secrets!?

Japanese: そうなんだ！　ここで見たことは、 おウチに帰っても、ヒミツだぞー。
English: Everything you see here is classified. No information can leave this building.

Japanese: はあい。
English: Understood!

Japanese: （なんだ？　この教育番組の おねえさんみたいなロボットは）
English: (Who is this woman? She's like a robot from some kind of whacked edumacational show...)

Japanese: おねえさんは、小池　ケイコって いうんだねー。
English: My name is Lisa Basil.

Japanese: この会社の、なんと 社長さんなんだよー。
English: I'm the company director.

Japanese: しゃ、シャチョーさん‥‥ですか！ （ニンゲンだったのか‥‥）
English: D-Director!? (Sh-She's human!? She seems more like a ghost in a shell.)

Japanese: （そういえば、岡　高夫と同じ 装置をつけているな‥‥）
English: (And that thing over her eye... Isn't that the same device as Glen Elg's?)

Japanese: 《おめがね》だっけ。
English: That's an DMH, right?

Japanese: 《モノクル》だよ！　‥‥字数しか 合ってないじゃないか！
English: Nice try, but it's the other way around, Maya. It's an HMD.

Japanese: バグダスのプログラマーは、 みーんなつけてるんだよー。
English: All of my programmers here at Blue Screens, Inc. are supplied with HMDs.

Japanese: 社長さんも作るんですか？ ぷろぐらむ。
English: Then do you write programs, too?

Japanese: おねえさんはねー。フンイキだけ 楽しんでるんだなー。
English: No. I just enjoy wearing this.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥あたしも ほしいもん。ちょっとだけ。
English: They ARE pretty cool! I wouldn't mind one!

Japanese: うわー、キップがいっぱいあるよ。
English: Wow! Look at this mess!

Japanese: それ、ぜんぶ馬券だね。
English: Looks like they're all betting tickets.

Japanese: ばけん？
English: What kind of betting tickets?

Japanese: 《勝ち馬投票券》。ケイバだよ。
English: For betting on which horse will win a given race. They're horse racing tickets.

Japanese: 引き出しにも入ってる。 ずいぶんいっぱいあるね‥‥
English: Oh, wow... His drawers are stuffed full of these...

Japanese: （これみんな、ハズレ馬券‥‥ みたいだな）
English: (Looks like they're all losing tickets, though.)

Japanese: 岡　高夫の《ハズレ馬券》を 拾い集めた。
English: Glen Elg's Losing Horse Racing Tickets gathered up.

Japanese: これだけあれば、 １００円ぐらいにはなるかもね。
English: This many tickets would get you... what? A buck down at the recycling center?

Japanese: なるほどくんもワルだねー。
English: You're hopeless, Nick. You know that?

Japanese: （そういうモノじゃ ないんだけどな。馬券‥‥）
English: (I'm just taking them as evidence, Maya!)

Japanese: うわ。ヒドい机だね、こりゃ。 どうよ、なるほどくん。
English: Whoa! Look at this desk, Nick! What a mess!

Japanese: ベツに‥‥フツウじゃないかな。
English: Looks pretty average to me.

Japanese: でも、シゴトにならないよね。 こんなに散らかってたら。
English: But you can't get any work done with everything all over the place like this.

Japanese: そうかな。デキるオトコは 仕事場を選ばないからね。
English: You think? Real whiz-kids can work under any condition, you know.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくの机を片づけ させようというつもりだろう）
English: (She's trying to hint that I should tidy my desk more.)

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、同意するもんか！）
English: (I'll clean up my desk when Maya stops asking silly questions... No hurry then.)

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 このカレンダー‥‥
English: Hey! This calendar...

Japanese: ‥‥？　机なら片づけないけど、 何かあった？
English: What about it...? If this is another hint about tidying, you can forget it.

Japanese: こ、これ‥‥。１２月　３日に、 赤くマークがついてるよ！
English: Someone's marked December 3rd in red pen!

Japanese: １２月　３日‥‥って、高夫さんが 殺害された日じゃないか！
English: December 3rd? That's the day Mr. Elg was murdered!

Japanese: 他に何か、書いてある？
English: Is there anything else?

Japanese: うん！　ええとね‥‥ 《ゼニトラに会う》だって。
English: Yeah. Umm... It says, "Meet with The Tiger."

Japanese: ぜ‥‥ゼニトラ‥‥
English: The T-Tiger?

Japanese: 《高夫のカレンダー》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Glen's Calendar added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あの‥‥。 この会社って、どういう？
English: So... What exactly is this firm's business?

Japanese: そうだねー。 わかりやすくセツメイすると‥‥
English: I will try to simplify it so that you can understand...

Japanese: ある企業が必要とするデータ管理 システムをブンセキして、
English: We analyze the data management systems required by certain branches of industry...

Japanese: ベストなオペレーションソフトと ソースの配信をしてるんだねー。
English: ...and deliver optimum operating system and source-level components.

Japanese: ‥‥意味わかんないね。
English: Huh? You lost me on the corner of Analyze and Management.

Japanese: いいよ。 フンイキだけつかんどけば。
English: It doesn't matter. They analyze stuff. You got that much, right?

Japanese: そうやって作ったプログラムを ＣＤにして、売っているんだよー。
English: The software we produce is distributed on CDs.

Japanese: し、しーでぃー‥‥ですか？
English: CDs...?

Japanese: そー、コンパクト・ディスク。 データをしまう、円盤だねー。
English: Yes. Compact disc. Digital optical storage media.

Japanese: （ＣＤって、音楽だけじゃなくて プログラムも記録できるからな）
English: (Of course, CDs are used for software as well as music...)

Japanese: 数は少ないけど、みんな スゴいプログラマーなんだぞー。
English: It is a small firm, but all of my employees are first- class programmers.

Japanese: ちょっと、 話を聞いてみようねー。
English: Let's ask one of them what they are doing.

Japanese: すみませーん。今、ナニを？
English: Excuse me. What are you working on right now?

Japanese: プログラムのストラクチャはコード の応答性がパフォーマンスの向上に
English: I'm researching the impact of time-slicing common areas in multi-threaded code

Japanese: ラジカルなグローバル性とメモリの オーバーヘッドの独立性が重要です
English: on logical access to shared global variables. Obviously, program structure heavily

Japanese: だからマルチスレッドにおける同じ ソースをタイムスライスされますが
English: influences response time and performance, so the co- independence of global

Japanese: 協調型のグローバル定数がラジカル に読みこむ利点を研究しています。
English: variables and memory overheads is vitally important to the success of the execution.

Japanese: ‥‥とまあ、 こんな感じなんだねー。
English: Well, you get the idea. This is the sort of thing we are involved in.

Japanese: よい子のみんなは、 わかったかなー？
English: Did you good people follow all of that?

Japanese: はははあ‥‥。
English: Y-Y-Yeah...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥うつろに ほほえんでるな）
English: (Your blank smile just said otherwise, Maya.)

Japanese: あの。岡　高夫さんですけど‥‥、
English: Um, about Mr. Elg...

Japanese: 何か、トラブルに 巻きこまれていたんですか？
English: Was he in some kind of trouble?

Japanese: んー？　どうして そう思うのかなー？
English: I'm sorry? Why would you think that?

Japanese: さっき、言いかけた じゃないですか。
English: I thought you said something about it just now?

Japanese: “ルーズな性格のせいで、 ちょっとしたトラブルに”って！
English: You said he got himself into trouble because he was a bit of a "loser".

Japanese: （サイコ・ロックか‥‥）
English: (A Psyche-Lock?)

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥ヒミツが あったんだね、岡　高夫さん。
English: I guess Mr. Elg is like every other man with his own pile of secrets.

Japanese: 聞かせてもらえませんか。 岡　高夫さんのトラブルのこと。
English: Please tell us more about the trouble that Mr. Elg was in.

Japanese: ‥‥さあねー。 おねえさんは知らないなー。
English: I'm sorry. I don't know anything about that.

Japanese: 私生活には、あんまりクビを つっこまなかったからねー。
English: I do not pry into other people's affairs.

Japanese: 今、はやりの“びじねすらいく” っていうヤツですね？
English: She just gave us the dreaded "professional, business-like cold-shoulder", huh?

Japanese: （被害者のトラブル‥‥ 知っておく必要があるな）
English: (I need to find out exactly what the victim was involved in.)

Japanese: （ロックを解除するか。 ‥‥ビジネスライクに！）
English: (Looks like I've got to break her in a "professional, business-like" manner!)

Japanese: 高夫くん、コンピュータよりも アタマがよかったんだけどねー。
English: Glen's head had more processing power than any computer.

Japanese: 《ギャンブル》っていうウイルスに 破壊されていたんだなー。
English: But it had been infected with a "gambling" virus.

Japanese: ‥‥高夫くん、追いつめられて いたんだねー。
English: Glen was... in too deep.

Japanese: 借金、ですか‥‥
English: You mean he was in debt?

Japanese: １０００万円だからねー。 返せないよねー、カンタンには。
English: Yes. 100,000 dollars in debt. Not an easy amount to repay.

Japanese: だから‥‥
English: So...

Japanese: 《危険なアルバイトをする》 ‥‥そう言ってたねー。
English: He said he was taking on some extra work. Something a bit risky.

Japanese: 《危険なアルバイト》‥‥？
English: "Risky"? How?

Japanese: 吐麗美庵のウエイトレスさん ‥‥かな。
English: Maybe he was going to become a waitress at Trés Bien!?

Japanese: （ちがうよ！）
English: (Where do you come up with these ideas!?)

Japanese: 社長さん、知ってますよね。 ‥‥事件のこと。
English: You know about what happened, right, Ms. Basil?

Japanese: 高夫くんが殺された、アレかなー？ カワイソウだったねー。
English: You mean about Glen being poisoned? Yes, I know. It's terrible.

Japanese: 何か、ご存じのこと、 ありませんか？
English: Can you tell us anything that might be helpful?

Japanese: うーん‥‥。警察のおじさんも 来たけどねー。
English: I don't think so. A police officer was here earlier, too.

Japanese: お役に立てることはなかったなー。
English: But I couldn't tell him anything either, because...

Japanese: ハンニンのウエイトレスさん、 会社とは関係なかったしねー。
English: ...the waitress who committed the crime has nothing to do with Blue Screens, Inc.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか。
English: Oh...

Japanese: あ。岡さんのデスク、もう かたづけちゃいましたか？
English: How about Mr. Elg's desk? Have you cleared it out already?

Japanese: それが、まだあるんだねー。 目の前の、コレなんだよー。
English: No, not yet. It's this one, right in front of you.

Japanese: ほしいものがあったら、 持ってっちゃっていいよー。
English: If there's anything that might be useful to you, you are welcome to take it.

Japanese: （手がかりになりそうなもの、 探してみるか‥‥？）
English: (I guess there might be a clue here somewhere...)

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥高夫さんが 作ったんですか。このウイルス。
English: So it's safe to say Mr. Elg was the creator of this virus, huh?

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》 ‥‥そうだねー。
English: The "MC Bomber" virus? Yes.

Japanese: よくないイミで、天才的な プログラムなんだよー。それ。
English: It was a work of genius. In a bad sort of way, of course. But still genius.

Japanese: 闇ルートでも、数億円の 値段がつくんじゃないかなー。
English: Something like that would probably fetch several million dollars on the black market.

Japanese: （めずらしく、イトノコ刑事の 言ったとおりだな‥‥）
English: (Inconceivable! Gumshoe was right for a change!)

Japanese: そのカレンダーに書いてある １２月　３日‥‥
English: This date, December 3rd, that is marked on his calendar...

Japanese: その日が、借金の支払い期限 だったんだねー。
English: That was his deadline for repaying his debts.

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》の データを書きなおした。
English: MC Bomber updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: （高夫さんの馬券‥‥ もう、いらないな）
English: (I guess we won't be needing these horse racing tickets any more.)

Japanese: 岡　高夫の《ハズレ馬券》を そっと床にもどした。
English: Glen Elg's Losing Horse Racing Tickets thrown back on the floor.

Japanese: ちゃんとゴミ箱に捨てる！
English: Use the trash can, Nick!

Japanese: あの。岡　高夫さんの ことですけど‥‥。
English: Um, about Mr. Elg...

Japanese: 優秀なプログラマーだったねー。 天才、っていうのかなー。
English: He was a top programmer. I would even say he was a genius.

Japanese: ちょーっと、性格に モンダイがあったけど。
English: But he did suffer from one or two bugs in his personality.

Japanese: 性格に‥‥？
English: Oh? Like what?

Japanese: ちょーっと、 ルーズだったんだねー。
English: He was a bit of a "loser"... Perhaps that would be the best way to describe it.

Japanese: そのせいで、ちょーっとした トラブルに‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: That's what got him into trouble...

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？
English: What's the matter?

Japanese: 優秀なプログラマーだったねー。 天才、っていうのかなー。
English: He was a top programmer. I would even say he was a genius.

Japanese: だから、なーんのモンダイも なかったんだねー。
English: So he was really no trouble at all. A model employee.

Japanese: いやいや！　さっき、たしかに‥‥
English: Hey, wait a minute!

Japanese: “ちょーっとしたトラブル” って‥‥
English: Just now you said something about him being in trouble!

Japanese: （聞き出す必要がありそうだな。 “トラブル”‥‥）
English: (We've got to find out what this trouble was exactly.)

Japanese: あの。‥‥ちょっとこれ、 見てもらえますか。
English: Umm, would you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: そのデータへのアクセスには パスワードが必要なんだねー。
English: I'm sorry. That data is SuPer-Admin Restricted Desktop Access password-protected.

Japanese: ぱすわーど？ なにそれ、なるほどくん。
English: SuPer-Admin Restricted Desktop Access password-protected!? What!? This is madness!

Japanese: 《合いコトバ》みたいな ものかな。
English: No, Maya, that is SPARDA. She won't tell us unless we say the right code word.

Japanese: 《合いコトバ》かあ。 ‥‥‥‥‥‥やま！
English: A code word? Hmm... Sesame!

Japanese: 《やま》と来たら《かわ》で キマリでしょ、ふつう‥‥
English: If it's not "sesame", then it must be her mother's maiden name. That's how it always is.

Japanese: それじゃ、パスワードのイミが ないからね。
English: There's no point in having a password if it's always the same thing, Maya.

Japanese: （‥‥しゃべる気はない、って コトか‥‥）
English: (I guess she just doesn't want to talk about this.)

Japanese: とりあえず、岡　高夫さんのコト だけでも聞いておこうか。
English: Maybe we should focus on asking about Glen Elg. What do you say?

Japanese: ～高夫のトラブル～
English: -- Glen's Trouble --

Japanese: さあ、聞かせてもらいましょうか。 岡　高夫さんの“トラブル”‥‥
English: So, how about you tell me what kind of trouble Mr. Elg was in?

Japanese: ザンネンだけど、“トラブル”は ここじゃ対応してないんだなー。
English: I'm sorry, sir, but we don't deal with trouble-shooting here.

Japanese: 《お客さま電話相談室》に 聞いてみてくれるかなー？
English: Perhaps you'd like to speak to someone in customer service?

Japanese: （ワケのわからない 対応をされた‥‥）
English: (What's she talking about!?)

Japanese: （岡　高夫の“トラブル”‥‥ なんとなく、見当はつくな）
English: (I guess I'd better just take a shot and see where it gets me...)

Japanese: ‥‥社長さん。 もしかして‥‥
English: Ms. Basil. Let me ask you something.

Japanese: これじゃないんですか？ 高夫さんの“トラブル”の原因。
English: Did Mr. Elg's troubles have something to do with this?

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか！
English: Well? What do you think!?

Japanese: どうかなー。ちょっと、 聞いてみようかなー。
English: I'm not sure. Let's ask one of the programmers...

Japanese: すみませーん。どうですかー？
English: Excuse me. Can you answer this man's query, please?

Japanese: トラブルシューティングはタスクの ジョブをキープするキューとデータ
English: The data queue that manages system tasks for trouble- shooting requires that the

Japanese: が必要なローカル変数をスリープに マルチタスクをシミュレートされる
English: multi-tasking simulation for local variables be put into sleep mode so that data

Japanese: シグナル送信とブロードキャストの アクティブなノードがバンドルする
English: transmission on active nodes can be bundled correctly according to the source code.

Japanese: わけですね。
English: ...Obviously.

Japanese: だそうです。よい子のみんな、 わかったかなー？
English: So I'm afraid that is the situation, you see. Did you good people follow all that?

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことか、さっぱり。
English: Not even slightly! What was all that mumbo-jumbo?

Japanese: ま、そういうことだねー。
English: It is exactly as my programmer explained.

Japanese: （今の証拠も見当ちがい、 ってことか‥‥）
English: (I'm guessing I picked the wrong piece of evidence there.)

Japanese: どうかなー、よい子のみんな？ まだ、やるのかなー？
English: Would you like to input another question?

Japanese: ナニかなー、それは。
English: What is that?

Japanese: 宝クジです。
English: A lottery ticket.

Japanese: 現場のテーブルに、 うなるほど落ちていました。
English: There were hundreds of these on the victim's table at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥その宝クジが どうかしたかなー？
English: And what is the relevance of that?

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫さん‥‥
English: The victim, Mr. Glen Elg...

Japanese: ギャンブル好き だったんじゃないですか？
English: ...had a gambling habit, didn't he?

Japanese: そんなクジだけじゃ、 なんとも言えないねー。
English: I don't think that's a logical conclusion based on the facts.

Japanese: 長者ラジオなら、おねえさんも 聞いてるんだよー。
English: Everyone dreams of being rich. Even I listen to Millionaire Radio.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥これだけじゃあ ちょっと、弱いかな‥‥）
English: (She's right. That is a bit weak on its own.)

Japanese: 高夫さんのギャンブル‥‥ 宝クジだけじゃないハズです！
English: But Mr. Elg's gambling wasn't restricted to the lottery, was it?

Japanese: ‥‥ギャンブル好きなのは、 よい子のみんなの方だったねー。
English: Everyone likes to gamble from time to time.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 気をつけた方がいいよー。
English: But be careful!

Japanese: ムチャなギャンブルは、おカネと 信用をハデに失うからねー。
English: If you're careless, you could lose everything -- your money, people's trust, etc.

Japanese: （‥‥カンタンなところで はずしたらしいな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I got that one wrong.)

Japanese: ナニかなー、それは。
English: What is that?

Japanese: 大量の馬券です。 しかも‥‥ハズレた馬券です。
English: A bunch of horse racing tickets. All losing ones.

Japanese: それだけあれば、１００円ぐらい 当たりがあるかもしれないねー。
English: With that many tickets, you could get 1 dollar at the recycling center.

Japanese: よい子のみんな、意外と ワルだなー。
English: You good people are very, very bad. Cashing in on others' misfortunes is immoral.

Japanese: （‥‥なんかムジュンしてないか？ 今のヒトコト‥‥）
English: (...Why did I get a whiff of hypocrisy just now?)

Japanese: でも‥‥その馬券が どうかしたかなー？
English: But what is the relevance of these tickets?

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫さん‥‥
English: The victim, Mr. Glen Elg...

Japanese: ギャンブル好き だったんじゃないですか？
English: He had a gambling habit, didn't he?

Japanese: そんな馬券だけじゃ、 なんとも言えないねー。
English: I don't think that's a logical conclusion based on the facts.

Japanese: ケイバなんて、 みんなフツウにやってるよー。
English: Everyone likes to go to the races from time to time.

Japanese: （ここまでやるヒトは “フツウ”じゃないと思うけど）
English: (Yeah, but not everyone buys this many tickets!)

Japanese: とにかく、それだけじゃ なんとも言えないねー。
English: Anyway, I don't believe that proves anything on its own.

Japanese: ‥‥それだけじゃないんですよ。
English: You're right. But I'm not through yet.

Japanese: 高夫さんのギャンブル‥‥ 馬券だけじゃないハズです！
English: Mr. Elg's gambling wasn't restricted to horse races, was it?

Japanese: ‥‥ギャンブル好きなのは、 よい子のみんなの方だったねー。
English: Everyone likes to gamble from time to time.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 気をつけた方がいいよー。
English: But be careful!

Japanese: ムチャなギャンブルは、おカネと 信用をハデに失うからねー。
English: If you're careless, you could lose everything -- your money, people's trust, etc.

Japanese: （‥‥カンタンなところで はずしたらしいな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I got that one wrong.)

Japanese: ‥‥宝クジ、ケイバ。それも、 どっさり買って、ばっさりハズレ。
English: The lottery, horse racing... He bought a lot of tickets, and lost a lot of times.

Japanese: かなり‥‥おカネが かかっていますよね。
English: That's got to have hurt his wallet pretty bad, don't you think?

Japanese: じゅうぶん“トラブル”の モトになるはずです！
English: Maybe bad enough to be the cause of some pretty serious trouble perhaps!?

Japanese: キャアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: Nooooooooo!

Japanese: たしかに高夫くん、ギャンブル 好きだったみたいだねー。
English: You are right. Glen did have a gambling habit.

Japanese: よい子のみんなは、 マネしちゃダメだぞー。
English: You good people must not follow his example, do you understand?

Japanese: マネしたくても、 おカネないですから。ぼくは。
English: Trust me. Even if I wanted to, I don't exactly have the money to buy any.

Japanese: でも‥‥。もうかっていれば 問題ないんじゃないかなー？
English: But... if you win, there's no problem, is there?

Japanese: たしかカレ、５０００万円 当てたんだよねー、宝くじで。
English: And Glen had a winning ticket, didn't he? For half a million dollars?

Japanese: そ、そうなんですけど。
English: Yeah, but...

Japanese: それじゃあ、トラブルとは 言えないんじゃないかなー？
English: It's hard to imagine how he could have been in trouble then, isn't it?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥最後のサイゴに、 高夫さんは５０００万円を当てた。
English: It's true that Mr. Elg won half a million dollars... in the end.

Japanese: しかし‥‥それまでは、 うまく行ってなかったはずです！
English: But that was his first stroke of good luck. He was in deep trouble before that.

Japanese: うまく行ってなかった‥‥？
English: Deep trouble? What do you mean?

Japanese: 岡　高夫は、おカネに困っていた。 ‥‥その証拠があります！
English: Mr. Elg's real problem was with someone or something more terrifying and ferocious!

Japanese: よい子のみんなも、あんまり うまく行ってないみたいだねー。
English: All good people get into trouble sometimes.
Huh?

Japanese: ちょっと、意見を 聞いてみようかなー？
English: Let's ask one of my programmers what they think...

Japanese: すみませーん。どうですかー？
English: Excuse me? What do you think?

Japanese: 休憩中。
English: I'm taking a break.

Japanese: おねえさんも、休憩しようかなー。
English: Yes. Perhaps I should take a break, too.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (I wish I could take a break from my troubles, but the rest stop was about a mile back.)

Japanese: 事件当日、高夫さんは ある人物に会っています。
English: Mr. Elg met with someone on the day he was killed.

Japanese: カレンダーにメモがある。 《ゼニトラに会う》‥‥と。
English: He even made a note on this calendar about the meeting: "Meet with The Tiger."

Japanese: それが、どうしたのかなー？
English: What is the relevance of that?

Japanese: 高夫くんが、借金の話でもしてた ‥‥と、こう言いたいのかなー？
English: Are you trying to suggest Glen was meeting him to discuss his debt?

Japanese: え、ええ。 まあ、そうなんですけど。
English: Y-Yeah. That's what I was thinking.

Japanese: でも‥‥その《ゼニトラ》さんが どんなヒトか、知らないからねー。
English: But I have never heard anything about this "Tiger" before.

Japanese: ニンゲンじゃなくて、 トラさんかもしれないねー。
English: Maybe he's not even human. Maybe he really is a tiger!

Japanese: （メチャクチャ言ってるな‥‥）
English: (I'm no programmer, but does she really expect me to buy such messed up logic?)

Japanese: なんだったら、紹介しましょう。 《ゼニトラ》というのは‥‥
English: In that case, I think it's time to introduce you to The Tiger.

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか！
English: Well? What do you think!?

Japanese: どうかなー。ちょっと、 聞いてみようかなー。
English: I'm not sure. Let's ask one of the programmers...

Japanese: すみませーん。どうですかー？
English: Excuse me. Can you answer this man's query, please?

Japanese: トラブルシューティングはタスクの ジョブをキープするキューとデータ
English: The data queue that manages system tasks for trouble- shooting requires that the

Japanese: が必要なローカル変数をスリープに マルチタスクをシミュレートされる
English: multi-tasking simulation for local variables be put into sleep mode so that data

Japanese: シグナル送信とブロードキャストの アクティブなノードがバンドルする
English: transmission on active nodes can be bundled correctly according to the source code.

Japanese: わけですね。
English: ...Obviously.

Japanese: だそうです。よい子のみんな、 わかったかなー？
English: So I'm afraid that is the situation, you see. Did you good people follow all that?

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことか、さっぱり。
English: Not even slightly! What was all that mumbo-jumbo?

Japanese: ま、そういうことだねー。
English: It is exactly as my programmer explained.

Japanese: （今の証拠も見当ちがい、 ってことか‥‥）
English: (I'm guessing I picked the wrong piece of evidence there.)

Japanese: 高夫くん、トモダチは たくさんいたからねー。
English: Glen had a lot of friends, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: “芝九蔵　虎ノ助”‥‥ローン会社 《カリヨーゼ》の社長です。
English: Furio Tigre... The boss of a loan office called "Tender Lender".

Japanese: ‥‥《カリヨーゼ》？ ダメっぽい社名だねー。
English: "Tender Lender"!? People with businesses should think harder before naming their offices.

Japanese: （ヒトのコト、言えるかよ） ‥‥で、どうですか？
English: (Like you're one to talk!) Well? What do you think?

Japanese: ウチは、それほど経営には 困ってないからねー。
English: Our firm is doing very well at the moment.

Japanese: エンリョしようかなー、借金は。
English: I don't think we need to borrow money.

Japanese: い、いやいや。 高夫さんのコトです。
English: No, no, no! I mean about Mr. Elg!

Japanese: その、芝九蔵ってヒトが、 高夫くんと関係あるのかなー？
English: You think Glen had something to do with this Furio Tigre?
Yes...

Japanese: ２人に、接点があったか どうかがわからないとねー。
English: I'm sorry. I don't know of any connection between the two of them.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥。被害者と ゼニトラの接点、か‥‥）
English: (Really? Because I've got proof that Mr. Elg and The Tiger knew each other.)

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか！
English: Well? What do you think!?

Japanese: どうかなー。ちょっと、 聞いてみようかなー。
English: I'm not sure. Let's ask one of the programmers...

Japanese: すみませーん。どうですかー？
English: Excuse me. Can you answer this man's query, please?

Japanese: トラブルシューティングはタスクの ジョブをキープするキューとデータ
English: The data queue that manages system tasks for trouble- shooting requires that the

Japanese: が必要なローカル変数をスリープに マルチタスクをシミュレートされる
English: multi-tasking simulation for local variables be put into sleep mode so that data

Japanese: シグナル送信とブロードキャストの アクティブなノードがバンドルする
English: transmission on active nodes can be bundled correctly according to the source code.

Japanese: わけですね。
English: ...Obviously.

Japanese: だそうです。よい子のみんな、 わかったかなー？
English: So I'm afraid that is the situation, you see. Did you good people follow all that?

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことか、さっぱり。
English: Not even slightly! What was all that mumbo-jumbo?

Japanese: ま、そういうことだねー。
English: It is exactly as my programmer explained.

Japanese: （今の証拠も見当ちがい、 ってことか‥‥）
English: (I'm guessing I picked the wrong piece of evidence there.)

Japanese: 高夫くん、トモダチは たくさんいたからねー。
English: Glen had a lot of friends, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ゼニトラ‥‥芝九蔵　虎ノ助は、 ローン会社の社長です。
English: Furio Tigre, aka "The Tiger", is the boss of a loan office called "Tender Lender".

Japanese: そんなオトコに、高夫さんは 個人的に会っているんですよ。
English: This is who Mr. Elg met with on the day of his murder.

Japanese: その話題は‥‥借金のコトに 決まっています！
English: And the only thing a loan shark would talk with him about would be his debt!

Japanese: キャアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: Noooooooo!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ギャンブルで 借金を作っちゃったみたいだねー。
English: It's true that Glen had racked up quite a bit of debt from his gambling habit.

Japanese: １０００万円ぐらいに なっていたみたいだよー。
English: About 100,000 dollars, I think.

Japanese: （１０００万円の借金、か‥‥）
English: (100,000 dollars? Ouch!)

Japanese: まあ。宝クジが当たったみたい だから、モンダイないねー。
English: But I heard he won the lottery, so he should have been in the clear.

Japanese: （その幸運‥‥ほんの少し、 マコちゃんにわけてほしいな）
English: (Shame Maggey couldn't get a bit of that good luck.)

Japanese: あれ‥‥でも。
English: OK...

Japanese: たまたまクジが当たったから いいようなものの‥‥
English: ...So the guy got lucky and won the lottery.

Japanese: もし、当たらなかったら‥‥
English: But what if he hadn't won?

Japanese: 高夫さん、どうするつもり だったんでしょう？　‥‥借金。
English: What was his plan then?

Japanese: それは‥‥言いにくいんだけど‥‥
English: Well... This isn't easy to say, but...

Japanese: “自分のウデで返す”‥‥ そう言ってたねー。
English: He said he would use his talents to repay the money.
His "talents"?

Japanese: プログラム、ということかなー。
English: I suspect he was talking about programming.

Japanese: い、１０００万円の値打ちがある プログラム‥‥ですか？
English: Wh-What computer program is worth 100,000 dollars!?

Japanese: よい子のみんな、そろそろ カンベンしてくれないかなー？
English: Perhaps you good people should leave so I can get back to my work.

Japanese: （そろそろ、トドメを 刺すときが来たな‥‥）
English: (I'm so close to cracking her!)

Japanese: その“プログラム”‥‥ もしかして、コイツですか？
English: The program in question... Was it by any chance... this!?

Japanese: ‥‥おねえさんにとっても、 デリケートなモンダイだからねー。
English: This is a very delicate matter.

Japanese: 決定的なデータがないかぎり、 アクセスはできないんだなー。
English: Without the necessary data, there's really no way I can access the information.

Japanese: （高夫さんは‥‥何かの プログラムを作ったんだ！）
English: (I know Mr. Elg created some sort of program!)

Japanese: （決定的証拠といえば‥‥ プログラム自体、か！）
English: (She can't deny it if I show her the program itself as evidence!)

Japanese: それは‥‥もちろん、 これですね。
English: Well? This is it, isn't it?

Japanese: 今、世間をコンランさせている コンピュータ・ウイルス‥‥
English: This is the virus that's infecting computers world-wide as we speak...

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》！
English: "MC Bomber"!

Japanese: キャアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: Noooooooo!

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: おお。こんぴゅーたーだよ なるほどくん！
English: Hey, look Nick! It's a super-computer!

Japanese: かしこそうだねー、見るからに。
English: It looks like it's really smart and wise, doesn't it?

Japanese: でも、コイツを作ったのは、 ぼくたち人間だからね。
English: Computers are only as smart as the humans who use them, Maya.

Japanese: でも、その“ぼくたち”の中に、 なるほどくんは入ってないよね。
English: That explains why we don't use the computer in our office!

Japanese: でも、それは真宵ちゃんも いっしょだろ。
English: You work there too, Maya.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: Yeah, but at least I'm...

Japanese: キミたち、バカなコトで ケンカしないでくれるかなー。
English: Please. Don't argue about something that's so trivial.

Japanese: コンピュータに笑われちゃうぞー。
English: Otherwise the computer will laugh at you.

Japanese: ケイコさんに笑われちゃうって、 なるほどくん。
English: She said she'd laugh at us, Nick.

Japanese: （だから、これは人間だよ！）
English: (She's a human, Maya, not a computer!)

Japanese: ワキ目もふらず、キーボードを カタカタたたいている。
English: He's really pounding that keyboard, isn't he?

Japanese: ひゃあ、すごいなあ。 ‥‥これが《ぷろ》なんだねえ。
English: Wow! I bet that's where the "pro" in "programmer" comes from, huh?

Japanese: やっぱり、これからは 手に職をつけないとダメだね。
English: I guess I shouldn't be resting on my laurels. Gotta expand my skill set and all that.

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: Yeah, that's a good idea.

Japanese: あたしも、マメのオジさんに 弟子入りしようかな。
English: Maybe I could become Old Seedy's apprentice.

Japanese: （霊媒師の修行は どうなっているんだろう‥‥）
English: (Um... And what about your spirit medium training?)

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、落ちつかない カタチをしてるな。
English: Those pillars almost look like they're moving. It's kind of unsettling.

Japanese: なんで、曲がってるのかな。 くにゃっ、て。
English: Nah, they just look a bit twisted or warped or whatever word I'm looking for...

Japanese: あー、このオフィスは 《未来》がテーマなんだねー。
English: This office was designed with a futuristic feel in mind.

Japanese: みらい‥‥？
English: Futuristic?

Japanese: 《未来的なデザイン》って なんだろう‥‥って考えて、
English: Yes. We tried to imagine what things might look like in the future when we designed it.

Japanese: このカタチに 落ちついたんだねー。
English: It helps to soothe and calm the soul.

Japanese: やっぱり、落ちつかないなあ。
English: On second thought, I agree with you, Nick. This place is really unsettling.
